title: Value constraints: support ranges and open-ended bounds (importer drops them)
description:
BUG + GAP (highest value). The metamodel's ValueConstraint is enumerated
values only -- Constraint.ts:81-88: `readonly values: readonly string[]`,
with the doc comment "Currently supports enumerated values."

Consequence in the importer: NormaXmlParser.ts:262 keeps a value range
ONLY when `minVal === maxVal`:

    if (minVal !== undefined && minVal === maxVal) { values.push(minVal); }

So every genuine ORM 2 value range -- `[1..10]`, `>= 18`, `(0..100)`,
mixed sets like `{1, 3, 5..9}` -- is **silently dropped on import**. Same
pattern at NormaXmlParser.ts:497-508 for role-level value constraints.

ORM 2 / NORMA value constraints support enumerations, closed ranges,
open/half-open ranges, and unions of these, with inclusive/exclusive
bounds. This is one of the most common conceptual constraints in real
models (ages, ratings, percentages, codes), so the loss is material.

Work: (a) extend ValueConstraint to a list of items where each item is a
literal OR a range with optional lower/upper bound and inclusivity flags;
(b) update NormaXmlParser to carry ranges through; (c) update validation
and verbalization; (d) round-trip fixture test. Keep `values: string[]`
working as a sugar/back-compat for pure enumerations.
acceptance_criteria: ValueConstraint can express inclusive/exclusive ranges, open-ended bounds, and mixed enumerations+ranges; NormaXmlParser imports MinValue!=MaxValue ranges and open bounds without loss; round-trip test over a NORMA value-range fixture; validation + verbalization handle ranges.
close_reason: Value ranges and open/exclusive bounds implemented: metamodel (ValueRange + ranges on ValueConstraint/ValueConstraintDef), serializer round-trip, JSON Schema, verbalization, population validation, and the NORMA importer (parser+mapper) which no longer drops MinValue!=MaxValue ranges. Tests added across core and formats; full monorepo build/test/lint green.

title: ORM 2 standard coverage audit: .orm.yaml/NORMA importer gaps
description:
Audit of conceptual ORM 2 coverage in the native `.orm.yaml` metamodel
(packages/core/src/model) and the NORMA XML importer
(packages/formats/src/Norma*), measured against the ORM 2 standard as
realized by NORMA. Goal: make barwise "play well with others" by closing
the genuine semantic divergences from NORMA before any export/round-trip
work.

Method: read each metamodel type and the NORMA parser/mapper, then
classify every candidate gap as REAL (file a child) or NOT-A-GAP
(already covered). Diagram/layout interop is intentionally out of scope
here (tracked separately by barwise-e3g) -- this epic is about *conceptual*
ORM 2 semantics only.

NOT gaps (verified covered, do not file):
- Preferred identifier: activated in NormaToOrmMapper.ts:375 (is_preferred).
- Unary/n-ary fact types: supported (FactType.ts:22-23, arity getter).
- Subtype partition (exclusive/exhaustive): SubtypeFact carries both flags.
- Implied mandatory constraints: intentionally filtered (NORMA auto-generated).
- Conceptual data types: 20+ NORMA types normalized to portable names.

REAL gaps (each a child of this epic):
1. Value ranges + open-ended value constraints (HIGH; also a silent
   import data-loss bug).
2. Derived fact types / derivation rules.
3. Deontic vs alethic constraint modality.
4. Object cardinality constraints (distinct from role frequency).
5. Independent (reference-independent) object types.
6. Set-comparison/ring constraints over join paths.
7. Default values on value types / roles.
8. Multi-role (role-sequence) frequency constraints.

Children 1-2 are the priority. Once the gap list is agreed, the
follow-on work is (a) a NORMA-vs-yaml comparison doc and (b) a spec for
NORMA export / round-trip; both are tracked separately.
notes:
Authoritative XSD census (2026-06-17) against ormsolutions/NORMA ORMModel/ObjectModel/ORM2Core.xsd confirmed the original (import-grounded) audit and surfaced constructs the code-grounded pass could not see (elements the importer never references). New children .9-.13 added. Minor verbalization/metadata not separately filed: IsPersonal (pronoun hint), RecognizedPhrases (verbalization phrases), IsExternal (externally-defined elements). Note: ORM2Core models value constraints as ValueTypeValueRestriction/RoleValueRestriction (not a *Constraint), which the importer already handles -- not a gap.
Cited deep-research pass (2026-06-17) compared the ORM2 book (Halpin & Morgan 2008; Halpin papers) against NORMA. Conclusion: ORM2Core.xsd is a faithful, near-superset realization of the book -- the gaps vs the book are TOOLING-completeness, not schema, in two spots: deontic modality (single-operator only, see .3) and formal derivation rules (partial, see .2). Methodology (CSDP, schema transformations, Rmap) is algorithms over models, not metamodel constructs, so 'missing' does not apply. Corrections to earlier chat claims: 'deep support for conceptual outer joins' was UNVERIFIED (inner joins confirmed; retract); semiderived('+') is NOT derived-and-stored('**'). Using ORM2Core.xsd as the completeness yardstick for STATIC STRUCTURAL ORM2 (the exporter's subset) is sound.

title: Entity-centric diagram layout overhaul
description: Rewrite the diagram layout engine to produce NORMA-style 2D layouts. Entity types are spatial anchors positioned via ELK stress algorithm; fact types are placed geometrically between their connected entities. Supports horizontal/vertical orientation, subtype fans, hub entities, and join constraints. See barwise/docs/specs/entity-centric-layout.spec.md for full spec.
notes: Feature complete and pushed on feature/entity-centric-layout branch. Commits: a705fb8 (initial stress layout), 79689ae (cluster-aware), 47ef199 (radial subtype), 8f98704 (spacing/edge routing), 8286e70 (drag), f2f784a (drag + orientation toggle), 07ff4e0 (FT drag persistence), 1a380bc (position persistence + sidebar), 92512bf/4005910 (focus/hop filter), 780cb31/fe91317 (views + ghost), cf6bf70 (objectification envelope collapse), 617cf02 (tool auto-resolve + live-refresh + pan/zoom preservation), b0cae15 (sidebar sort). Verification: npm run build OK, npm run lint OK (0 errors, 18 pre-existing warnings), npm run test 1,990/1,990 pass across 7 packages. Remaining work before merging to main is organizational: decide whether to open a PR or merge directly.

title: Map diagnostics to YAML source positions instead of line 0
description: Currently DiagnosticsProvider hard-codes all diagnostic ranges to line 0, character 0, which causes yellow squiggly lines to appear under orm_version instead of the actual problematic objects/facts. The core Diagnostic objects include an elementId (UUID) but the provider ignores it. Fix requires: (1) YAML source mapping during deserialization so element IDs can be traced back to line/character positions, (2) DiagnosticsProvider to use elementId to look up the correct range, (3) fallback to document start for project-level diagnostics that don't target a specific element.
close_reason: Implemented YamlSourceMap and updated DiagnosticsProvider to resolve validation diagnostics to exact YAML source positions. PR #54.

title: Mandate identifier fact types in extraction prompt
description: GPT-5 Mini skips explicit identifier fact types (e.g. 'Patient has MedicalRecordNumber') even when entities have reference_modes. This causes downstream constraint skips because constraints reference fact types that don't exist. The prompt must explicitly instruct: for every entity with a reference_mode, emit a binary fact type 'Entity has Value' with is_preferred:true uniqueness on the entity role, reverse uniqueness on the value role, and mandatory on the entity role. This is the single highest-impact improvement for extraction quality.
close_reason: Resolved in v0.2.1 prompt (PR #51). Identifier fact types now mandated; all three transcripts produce them correctly.

title: Ternary fact type uniqueness should span Order+Product roles
description: In 'Order contains Product in Quantity', the uniqueness constraint is only on the Order role, meaning each Order contains at most one thing total. The transcript says 'Each line in the order specifies a product and a quantity' -- uniqueness should span Order+Product roles (each Order-Product combination has at most one Quantity). The LLM is not correctly identifying composite uniqueness for ternary fact types.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. Mini now produces composite uniqueness on Order+Product roles in ternary fact type.

title: Missing reverse uniqueness on reference-mode fact types
description: Reference-mode fact types (e.g., 'Customer has CustomerId') only get uniqueness on the entity role but not on the value role. The second constraint (each CustomerId identifies at most one Customer) is needed to make the identifier a bijection. The LLM recognizes the constraint conceptually (see NOTE annotations) but emits a duplicate of the entity-side constraint instead of creating a value-side constraint. May be an LLM prompt issue or a parser mapping issue.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. All identifier fact types now have both entity-role and value-role uniqueness constraints.

title: Customer places Order: uniqueness on wrong role
description: In the order-management transcript extraction, 'Customer places Order' has uniqueness on the Customer role (each Customer places at most one Order) instead of the Order role (each Order is placed by at most one Customer). The transcript clearly states 'Every order belongs to exactly one customer, and customers can place multiple orders.' This is a semantic inversion error in LLM extraction. Investigate whether the extraction prompt needs clearer guidance on which role to constrain for many-to-one relationships.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. Customer places Order now has uniqueness on Order role (each Order placed by one Customer).

title: Duplicate is_preferred constraints on reference-mode fact types
description: LLM extraction produces duplicate internal_uniqueness constraints with is_preferred: true on the same role in reference-mode fact types (e.g., 'Customer has CustomerId' has two identical constraints on the entity role). The parser should deduplicate constraints with identical type, roles, and is_preferred flag. Observed in order-management.orm.yaml lines 104-111, 127-134, 150-157.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.2.2 prompt. No duplicate is_preferred constraints in any v0.2.2 outputs.

title: Add VS Code integration tests
description: packages/vscode/tests/ is empty. Add integration tests using @vscode/test-electron covering: diagnostics rendering, command execution, diagram panel opening, completion behavior, and transcript import flow.
close_reason: Merged via PR #23 -- 15 unit tests + 7 integration tests for barwise-vscode

title: Implement objectified fact types
description: Add ObjectifiedFactType to the metamodel. Objectified fact types (nested fact types) are fact types reified as entity types so they can participate in other relationships. Specified in ARCHITECTURE.md Section 3.1.1. Requires changes across model, serialization, validation, verbalization, diagram, and schema.
close_reason: Merged via PR #22 -- objectified fact types implemented across all packages with 560 tests passing

title: Book-verify: read 3rd ed. serialization (JSON/YAML) coverage (CC5)
description: The 3rd ed. (2024) newly covers JSON/YAML/markup serialization of ORM models -- barwise's exact domain. Read it and check whether the book sanctions a serialization framing the native .orm.yaml should align to. See docs/book-verification-checklist.md CC5.
notes:
BOOK QUESTIONS (3rd ed., 2024) to answer from the serialization / data-file-format / NoSQL chapters. Each maps to a barwise .orm.yaml choice to verify. barwise format: root keys {orm_version, model}; model = {name, domain_context, note, object_types[], fact_types[] (constraints INLINE), subtype_facts[], objectified_fact_types[], populations[], definitions[], diagrams[]}; roles/object-types referenced by opaque id.
Q1 Does the book show a worked JSON and/or YAML serialization of an ORM model (not just relational DDL)? Which listings/figures?
Q2 CRUX: is that JSON/YAML the CONCEPTUAL ORM model serialized, or the PHYSICAL target schema for a document/NoSQL DB the model maps TO? Does the book keep conceptual vs physical separate (ours: .orm.yaml is conceptual; DDL is derived)?
Q3 Top-level structure: separate object-type and fact-type collections (like ours), or organized differently?
Q4 Constraints INLINE on the fact type, or in a separate id-referenced section (NORMA-style)? [our key anti-NORMA choice]
Q5 Keyed by opaque ids + id-refs (id graph, NORMA-style), or by names/readings as primary keys?
Q6 How are fact-type readings represented (placeholder templates like '{0} ... {1}'?)
Q7 Are sample populations (fact instances) in the same file as the schema, or separate (CSV for data)? [we embed populations]
Q8 Does the conceptual serialization include diagram/layout/presentation info, or keep it out? [we embed diagrams]
Q9 Any schema/format VERSION field (like our orm_version)?
Q10 Derivation rule serialized as text or formal expression? Join/set-comparison path serialized as root+roles+projection?
Q11 Does the book name a canonical ORM interchange format, or treat NORMA .orm XML as the standard? Is JSON/YAML offered as interchange or just illustration?

title: Book-verify: cardinality unary-role restriction (T2.1)
description: Confirm the book's cardinality constraints and whether restricting role cardinality to unary roles is correct or too narrow (the unary split was read off the NORMA XSD). See docs/book-verification-checklist.md.

title: Book-verify: frequency role-sequence + min floor (T2.3)
description: Confirm the book permits frequency over a role sequence with tuple semantics, and whether min>=1 is a book rule or a tooling default we carried. See docs/book-verification-checklist.md.

title: Book-verify: deontic = warning rendering (T1.6)
description: Confirm against Halpin's modality treatment that a deontic violation is faithfully a recorded/permitted state (our warning, not error), the per-constraint tag is correct, and excluding compound modal operators is sound. See docs/book-verification-checklist.md.

title: Book-verify: verbalization FORML fidelity (CC1)
description: Audit every constraint verbalizer against ORM2-02 FORML2; several emit deterministic approximations (e.g. join 'project the same [Person, Country]' vs Halpin's relative-clause form). Decide per family whether to move toward FORML. See docs/book-verification-checklist.md.

title: Book-verification audit (anti-NORMA-centrism)
description: Standing audit that the Tier 1-3 metamodel constructs follow Halpin's book, not NORMA's tooling. Full enumerated list lives in docs/book-verification-checklist.md; child issues track the high-value verifications. Resolve items in the doc as they are checked.

title: Value-comparison constraints (role-to-role value comparison)
description:
GAP (confirmed against ORM2Core.xsd: ValueComparisonConstraintType).
ORM 2 supports value-comparison constraints asserting an ordering
relationship between the values of two roles, e.g. "Employee.startDate <
Employee.endDate" or "Sale.price >= Sale.cost". Operators: <, <=, =, <>,
>=, >. This is distinct from ring constraints (which relate two roles
played by the same object type by instance identity, not value order).

Neither the metamodel nor the importer has any notion of it (grep for
ValueComparisonConstraint in packages/formats is empty), so NORMA models
using it lose the constraint silently. This was invisible to the original
import-grounded audit because the importer never references the element.

Work: add a value-comparison constraint kind (two role ids + operator);
import ValueComparisonConstraint; verbalize; validate against populations.
acceptance_criteria: Metamodel can express a value-comparison constraint between two roles with an operator (<, <=, =, <>, >=, >); importer reads ValueComparisonConstraint; verbalization renders it; not conflated with ring constraints.
notes: Core construct landed (PR pending): model (ValueComparisonConstraint + operator union + isValueComparison guard), serializer round-trip, JSON Schema branch, verbalization (operator phrases), population validation (numeric/lexical), diff key, and the one-time orm_version 1.0 -> 1.1 bump (CURRENT_ORM_VERSION + schema const + no-op forward migration) per ADR-0001. Scope: v1 compares two roles of the SAME fact type; join-path value comparison is deferred to the role-path model (barwise-0s8/5t9.10). NORMA importer/exporter mapping deferred to a follow-on (RT-B fidelity) -- NORMA's ValueComparisonConstraint operator enum needs XSD verification. Leaving open until the importer lands.

title: Implement NORMA XML export (model -> .orm) per spec
description:
Add the NORMA exporter -- the inverse of the existing importer -- as an
ExportFormatAdapter on the `normaFormat` descriptor in @barwise/formats,
so `barwise export --format norma` and the MCP export tool light up
through the registry.

Design and rationale: barwise/docs/specs/norma-export.spec.md. Key
framing: NORMA is a superset of barwise's metamodel, so model -> NORMA is
lossless for the representable subset today; barwise-5t9 bounds only the
NORMA -> model -> NORMA round-trip (RT-B), not the exporter.

Workstreams:
- WS1 semantic exporter + RT-A (model -> NORMA -> model) round-trip. Now.
- WS2 diagram geometry emission. Gated on barwise-e3g.
- WS3 RT-B fidelity as each barwise-5t9 construct becomes representable.
design: See barwise/docs/specs/norma-export.spec.md. WS1 (semantic exporter + RT-A round-trip) is unblocked and shippable now; WS2 (diagram geometry) is gated on barwise-e3g; WS3 (RT-B fidelity) grows as barwise-5t9 items land.
acceptance_criteria: normaFormat descriptor gains an exporter; barwise export <src> --format norma produces a .orm that re-imports equal to the source (RT-A) across tests/fixtures/*.orm; reference schemes re-expanded; implied constraints not emitted; no core change.

title: Object cardinality constraints (population size), distinct from role frequency
description:
GAP. barwise has FrequencyConstraint (Constraint.ts:216-231) which bounds
how many times an object plays a *role* ("each Customer places 2..5
Orders"). ORM 2 also has CARDINALITY constraints that bound the *number
of instances* of an object type (or the number of instances playing a
role): "there are at most 50 Departments", "between 2 and 10 active
Promotions". These are different concepts and NORMA models them
separately.

There is no way to express object-population cardinality today, so such
constraints are lost on import and unrepresentable in authored models.

Work: add a cardinality constraint kind keyed on an object type (and
optionally a role), with min/max (max may be unbounded); wire import,
verbalization, validation against populations.
acceptance_criteria: Metamodel can express a cardinality bound on an object type's population (and optionally on a role's instance count); importer reads NORMA CardinalityConstraint; verbalization renders it; not conflated with FrequencyConstraint.
notes: Core construct (WS1-WS3) implemented: shared CardinalityRange; ObjectType.cardinality field + unary CardinalityConstraint union member; round-trip + JSON Schema; population-gated validation (object-type count via buildObjectUniverse, unary-role count via valuesPlayedInRole; deontic -> warning); unary arity-1 structural check; verbalization (both forms); diff. WS4 NORMA mapping deferred -> see follow-up issue.

title: Derived fact types and derivation rules
description:
GAP. No derivation concept exists in the metamodel (rg for
deriv/derived across core/src/model returns only an unrelated comment in
SubtypeFact.ts). ORM 2 supports derived predicates -- purely derived
(computed on demand) and derived-and-stored -- each carrying a
derivation rule (formal/relational or natural-language). Subtype-defining
rules are the same family (a subtype defined by a predicate rather than
asserted).

Today every fact type in `.orm.yaml` is asserted/base; a modeler cannot
say "Person is an Adult iff Person has Age >= 18" or "TotalPrice is
derived from Quantity * UnitPrice." NORMA models that use derivation lose
that intent on import.

Work: add an optional derivation field to FactType (and a defining-rule
option to SubtypeFact), decide the rule representation (start with a
free-text/natural-language rule string + a derivation kind enum), wire
import where NORMA expresses it, and verbalize. Keep formal rule grammar
out of scope for v1.
acceptance_criteria: Metamodel can mark a fact type as derived (purely-derived or derived-and-stored) and attach a derivation rule; importer reads NORMA derivation rules where present; verbalization renders the rule; validation treats derived facts appropriately.
notes: Core construct (WS1-WS3) implemented: DerivationRule/DerivationKind/DerivationStorage; FactType.derivation + SubtypeFact.definingRule; round-trip + JSON Schema; derivation consistency validation (blank-rule warning, purely-derived-with-population warning); verbalization (fact-type derivation sentence + subtype defining-rule clause); diff (derivation change on fact types). Subtype-fact diffing does not exist in the codebase, so the spec's 'defining_rule into subtype diff' line is N/A (noted in PR). WS4 NORMA mapping deferred -> follow-up issue.

title: Deontic vs alethic constraint modality
description:
GAP. No modality concept exists (rg for deontic/alethic across
core/src/model returns nothing). ORM 2 distinguishes ALETHIC constraints
(logical necessity -- cannot be violated in any valid population, the
default) from DEONTIC constraints (obligation -- should hold, but
violations are recorded rather than impossible). NORMA renders these
differently (solid vs dashed/violet, "must/may" vs "should/should not").

Currently all barwise constraints are implicitly alethic, so deontic
business rules ("a manager should not approve their own expense") cannot
be expressed and are lost on import.

Work: add an optional `modality: "alethic" | "deontic"` to the shared
constraint shape (default alethic), thread it through import,
verbalization (modal verb selection), and validation (deontic violations
are warnings/recorded, not errors).
acceptance_criteria: Every constraint can carry a modality (alethic default | deontic); importer reads NORMA modality; verbalization uses the correct modal wording (must/may vs should/should not); default remains alethic for back-compat.
notes: Implemented: shared ConstraintBase carries modality?; serializer round-trips (deontic-only emission); JSON Schema enum on all branches; verbalization prefixes 'It is obligatory that'; deontic population violations are warnings via severityForModality; diff key folds modality. DEFERRED: NORMA Modality=Deontic import/export pending ORM2Core.xsd verification (same caution as other deferred NORMA mappings).
close_reason: Implemented and pushed in PR #219

title: Symbolic model query API in @barwise/core
description:
Build a deterministic, programmatic query interface over the ORM metamodel. Treats the model as a queryable graph of object types, fact types, roles, and constraints.

This is foundational: it makes the @barwise chat agent cheaper and more trustworthy (most 'questions about the model' become structured queries, not LLM prompts), it gives derivation rules a target language ('a derivation rule is just a named query'), and it gives CLI/MCP a stable surface for model introspection.

Examples of questions it must answer deterministically:
- What fact types involve <object type>?
- What constraints reference <role>?
- What is downstream / upstream of objectifying this fact?
- What object types have no mandatory roles? (model-quality query)
- What fact types share roles with <fact type>?

Interface surface:
- @barwise/core: new public API (likely src/query/ alongside validation, verbalization)
- CLI: new 'barwise query' command, or extend 'barwise show' subcommands
- MCP: new query_model tool exposing the API
- VS Code: chat agent (@barwise) delegates to this API for deterministic questions before falling back to LLM
acceptance_criteria: Public API documented; CLI subcommand works; MCP tool registered; chat agent uses it for at least 5 representative deterministic queries

title: OWL/SHACL import/export connector
description:
Add a format connector for the W3C semantic-web stack: OWL for class hierarchies and property characteristics, SHACL for closed-world data validation.

Per discussion comparing conceptual modeling approaches: ORM2's constraint vocabulary maps closely to SHACL's, and ORM2 + SHACL is the strongest combination on the page that no current tool ships. This connector reframes Barwise from 'ORM2 tool' to 'fact-oriented front-end to the W3C stack'.

Plugs into the existing FormatDescriptor registry in @barwise/core (packages/core/src/format/).

Interface surface:
- CLI: 'barwise export --format=owl|shacl', 'barwise import --format=owl'
- MCP: existing export_model / import_model tools pick up the new format automatically via the registry
- VS Code: register format in import/export commands and quick-pick

Mapping notes:
- ORM uniqueness -> sh:unique
- ORM mandatory / cardinality -> sh:minCount / sh:maxCount / owl cardinality restrictions
- ORM ring constraints -> owl property characteristics (subset) + SHACL-SPARQL for the rest
- ORM subtypes -> rdfs:subClassOf / owl:disjointWith
- ORM objectified facts -> intermediate resource pattern
acceptance_criteria: OWL+SHACL output validates with pySHACL; round-trip preserves ORM constraints that have direct OWL/SHACL analogs; CLI, MCP, and VS Code can all use the format

title: MCP diagram test fixture: Customer vs Client mismatch
description: The MCP diagram tool test references an entity named 'Customer' in packages/mcp/tests/fixtures/simple.orm.yaml, but the fixture currently defines an entity named 'Client'. The test fails because the expected element is missing. During the previous session I tried to edit the fixture to use 'Customer' but the edit was reverted by an unknown mechanism (possibly a hook, possibly a generator script) each time. Need to: (1) identify what is reverting edits to this fixture, (2) decide whether the fixture or the test is the source of truth, (3) fix whichever side is wrong. Current state: 1 pre-existing MCP test failure in the diagram suite.
acceptance_criteria: packages/mcp tests pass with no skipped/failing diagram tests; the source-of-truth decision is documented in the fix commit.
close_reason: Root cause was test isolation, not fixture drift. The merge tool auto-writes the merged YAML back to the base file path (merge.ts:94-97), so running the merge test overwrote packages/mcp/tests/fixtures/simple.orm.yaml with the content of simple-modified.orm.yaml on every run. Fixed merge.test.ts to copy the base fixture to a tmp file before each test so the committed fixture is not mutated. All 76 MCP tests now pass from a clean state.

title: Verify and wire up .orm-project.yaml support in CLI, MCP, and VS Code
description: Audit the current state of .orm-project.yaml support across the user-facing surfaces and fill gaps. Today the core package can load a project and resolve cross-domain refs, but it is unclear whether (a) the barwise CLI commands (validate, verbalize, diagram, export, diff) accept a .orm-project.yaml as input, (b) the MCP generate_diagram / validate / verbalize tools accept a project manifest, (c) the VS Code diagram command and DiagramPanel can open a project, and (d) the LSP registers .orm-project.yaml as a handled file type. For any surface that does not support it, decide whether to add support now or document the limitation. This blocks the splitting guide because the guide needs to tell the user how to actually use the result.
acceptance_criteria: Written audit of current project-file support across CLI/MCP/VS Code/LSP; each gap either fixed or tracked as a follow-up issue; at minimum 'barwise validate path/to/project.orm-project.yaml' and 'barwise diagram path/to/project.orm-project.yaml --domain catalog' work.

title: OrmProject multi-file workflow end-to-end
description: Epic: make the OrmProject multi-file workflow a first-class, usable feature. Today the metamodel, serializers, and schemas exist (see ARCHITECTURE.md §3.1.4 and packages/core/tests/integration/fixtures/multi-domain/) but there is no user-facing path to get from a monolithic .orm.yaml to a working multi-file project. This epic covers the docs, CLI tooling, and VS Code/MCP integration needed to close that gap. Worked example should be splitting auction.orm.yaml into catalog/auctions/payments/parties domains with crossing context mappings.
acceptance_criteria: User can start from a single .orm.yaml, run a command to scaffold a project, open the resulting .orm-project.yaml in VS Code, and generate a diagram of a single domain or the full project.

title: Add user-defined custom attributes to model elements
description: Allow users to attach arbitrary key/value metadata to ObjectType, FactType, Role, constraints, SubtypeFact, OrmModel, and OrmProject so they can customize models for purposes we haven't anticipated (subdomain tagging, ownership, external IDs, review status, etc.). Must round-trip through YAML serialization, update JSON schemas, and be surfaced in diff output. Custom keys should not trigger validation errors. Provides substrate for barwise-un8 (subdomain categorization).
design: Start with flat Record<string, string|number|boolean>; reserve 'barwise.*' namespace; serializer round-trip tests required; diff engine must compare custom fields.
acceptance_criteria: Round-trip tests pass for all element types; JSON schema updated; diff surfaces custom changes; validation ignores unknown keys; MCP tool outputs include custom field.

title: Show neighbors: ghost node preview for saved views
description: Right-click entity in diagram -> Show neighbors reveals 1-hop neighbors as ghost nodes (dimmed, dashed). Click ghost to add to view. Clear preview button to dismiss. Enables two-step explore-then-commit workflow for building saved views.

title: Click-to-highlight entity connections in diagram
description: Clicking an entity in the diagram highlights its direct connections: connected fact types, edges, and partner entities. Provides immediate visual feedback about an entity's neighborhood without changing the view. Shift-click to multi-select for creating views later.
close_reason: Implemented click-to-highlight in both sidebar tree and diagram. Clicking an element dims unrelated nodes/edges and highlights the element with its direct connections. Context menus added for Highlight in Diagram and Copy Name.

title: Sidebar model browser tree view
description: VS Code sidebar tree view showing the model structure: entity types, fact types, subtype hierarchies, constraints. Clicking an element highlights it in the active diagram. Foundation for view management -- later phases add the ability to create/switch views from this panel.
close_reason: Implemented sidebar model browser tree view with activity bar icon, ModelTreeProvider showing entity types, value types, fact types with constraints, subtype relationships, and diagram layouts. Auto-refreshes on file switch and save.

title: Persist diagram positions in .orm.yaml
description: Save user-arranged diagram positions in the orm.yaml file so layouts survive across sessions. The coordinate system should be simple and human-editable: integer pixel coordinates with (0,0) at top-left, x increasing right, y increasing down. No fractional inches (NORMA style) or internal padding offsets. A diagrams section in the YAML with per-element x,y positions and optional width/height overrides. Users should be able to hand-edit positions without confusion. This is the foundation for multi-view support (barwise-dks).
close_reason: Implemented diagram position persistence in .orm.yaml: DiagramLayout on OrmModel, JSON schema, serializer round-trip, Save button in webview, auto-load on open. Integer pixels, name-keyed, screen coordinates.

title: ORM meta-model of diagram layout rules
description: Create a .orm.yaml model that formalizes the diagram layout rules using ORM itself. Transcript saved at examples/transcripts/diagram-layout.transcript.txt. Import via barwise MCP or CLI, validate, generate diagram. Covers: cluster detection, subtype radial placement, leaf distribution, fact type stacking, boundary entity nudging.

title: Draggable entity positions in diagram webview
description: Make entity types draggable in the VS Code webview SVG. When dragged, re-run Pass 2 (fact type placement + edge routing) with the new entity position and re-render. Optionally persist manual positions in a companion .orm-layout.yaml file. Foundation for verbalization-on-selection and click-to-focus features.
close_reason: Drag-to-reposition entities and double-click to toggle fact type orientation both implemented and pushed

title: Interactive diagram: click-to-focus subset view
description: Add click handlers to SVG entity types in the VS Code webview. When an entity is clicked, show a toolbar with hop selector (1/2/3/All). Re-generate diagram with only the selected entity's N-hop neighborhood. Maps to Looker Explore concept: each entity-centric view is like an Explore centered on a fact table. Requires filterGraph() in @barwise/diagram and webview message passing.
close_reason: Implemented click-to-focus subset view. Double-click entity to enter focus mode with hop selector (1/2/3/All). N-hop neighborhood BFS filter computes connected entities through fact types and subtype relationships. Clear button returns to full model.

title: Radial subtype placement
description: Subtypes should radiate outward from the diagram center relative to their supertype. The fan direction is determined by the vector from diagram center through the supertype position. Subtypes with fewer connections belong at the periphery. Replace the current rigid 'subtypes below' rule with this radial approach.
close_reason: Fixed stale object reference bug in normalizeCoordinates and removed redundant second placeSubtypesRadially call

Add unit tests with mock LlmClient covering: provider selection, model override, error handling for API failures, and verification that extraction features (all 11 constraints, objectification, aliases) flow through correctly.

Check that the handler logic in DraftModelParser.ts has no untested branches.

title: Extract role-level value constraints from transcripts
description: DraftModelParser currently skips role-level value constraints with the message 'Role-level value constraints from transcripts are not yet supported.' These are value restrictions on a role within a fact type (as opposed to on the value type itself), e.g. 'in the context of grading, only grades A-F are allowed'. The prompt should instruct the LLM to emit these, and the parser should apply them.
close_reason: Implemented role-level value constraint extraction in DraftModelParser. LLM can now emit values array in inferred_constraints, parser resolves role and applies constraint. PR #55.

title: Show transcript name and LLM model in extraction progress message
description: When extracting a transcript, the VS Code progress message says 'Extracting ORM model from transcript...' which is generic. It should include the transcript filename and the LLM model being used, e.g. 'Extracting ORM model from transcript clinic-appointments.md with model gpt-5-mini...'. This gives the user better feedback, especially when comparing extraction quality across models.
close_reason: Closed

title: Disallow fabricated reference modes in extraction prompt
description: GPT-5 Mini sometimes invents reference_mode values like 'auto_counter (generated OrderLineId)' or 'CourseCode + TermCode (composite of Course and Term) - inferred' which are not valid ORM notation. The prompt should forbid composite or generated reference modes. If identification is unclear, flag it as an ambiguity rather than fabricating a scheme.
close_reason: Resolved in v0.2.1 prompt (PR #51). No fabricated reference modes in v0.2.1 or v0.2.2 outputs.

title: Require data type lengths on text fields in extraction prompt
description: GPT-5.3 Codex often omits length on text data types while GPT-5 Mini includes them. The prompt should instruct: always provide a length for text data types. Infer reasonable lengths from context (codes 10-20, names 100-200, free text 500+). This ensures the generated schema is more complete for downstream DDL generation.
close_reason: Resolved in v0.2.1 prompt (PR #51). Both Mini and Codex include data type lengths on all text fields.

title: Strengthen ternary/n-ary fact type guidance in extraction prompt
description: When transcripts describe constraints spanning 3+ roles (e.g. 'a patient can only have one appointment per time slot on a given day'), GPT-5 Mini fails to model these as multi-role fact types with appropriate uniqueness constraints. The prompt should instruct the LLM to recognize scheduling/composite uniqueness patterns and emit proper n-ary fact types rather than leaving them as prose TODOs.
close_reason: Resolved in v0.2.2 prompt (PR #53). Mini now produces 5-ary scheduling fact type with multiple uniqueness constraints.

title: Batch transcript import to reduce Copilot premium model quota usage

Could be exposed as: multi-select in VS Code file picker, a CLI command accepting multiple files, or a combined prompt that extracts multiple models in one LLM call. Key consideration: need to evaluate whether a single combined prompt produces comparable quality to individual extractions, and whether the Copilot API supports long enough context windows for multiple transcripts.
close_reason: Merged in PR #56. Batch import command implemented.

title: Add verbose LLM interaction logging to VS Code extension
description: Add a BARWISE.verboseLogging setting that controls detailed LLM interaction logging in the VS Code output channels. When enabled, log: prompt length and model config, raw JSON response (or truncated), token usage and latency (if reported by provider), full extraction timing. Applies to both Import Transcript and Import dbt commands. Currently the output channels only show post-extraction summaries (ambiguities, warnings, skipped constraints) with no visibility into the LLM call itself.
close_reason: Closed

title: Diagram layout: minimize line overlap and distance between connected nodes
description: Current ELK layout places all object types in one horizontal row and fact types in another, causing long crossing connector lines. Observed in order-management-diagram.png: Product connects across full width to 'Order has OrderStatus', Order connects far right to 'Customer places Order'. Goals: (1) minimize edge crossings, (2) place fact type boxes near their participating object types, (3) reduce total connector length. Investigate ELK layout options: layered algorithm with crossing minimization, port constraints, node ordering strategies.
close_reason: Closed

title: Incremental model update workflow for breaking changes
description: When importing a second conversation transcript (same or different stakeholders), the new information may conflict with or override existing domain knowledge. We need a workflow that: (1) detects breaking changes (renamed entities, removed fact types, changed constraints), (2) presents them clearly to the modeler with before/after context, (3) allows selective acceptance/rejection of each change, and (4) preserves audit trail of why changes were made. The existing diff/merge infrastructure (diffModels, mergeModels, reviewDeltas in ImportTranscriptCommand) handles additive changes but needs to be extended for breaking change scenarios -- e.g. when a fact type's arity changes, when an entity is split or merged, or when a constraint is reversed.
close_reason: All 6 stages complete and merged

title: Annotate .orm.yaml from transcript import with actionable clarification TODOs
description:
When the LLM extracts a model from a transcript, inject TODO(BARWISE) and NOTE(BARWISE) comments directly into the generated .orm.yaml file based on ambiguities, skipped constraints, and missing information.

Instead of generic 'this was ambiguous' flags, each TODO should frame a specific follow-up question the engineer can take back to their domain expert:

- Missing reference mode: 'Ask: How do you uniquely identify a Customer?'
- Unclear cardinality: 'Ask: Can a Customer place multiple Orders, or only one?'
- Possible value constraint: 'The transcript mentioned pending/shipped/delivered -- ask: Are those the only valid statuses?'
- Skipped constraint: 'Sounded mandatory but could not confirm direction -- ask: Must every Order have a Customer?'

Data sources already available in DraftModelResult:
- ambiguities[] (with description and source_references)
- warnings[]
- constraintProvenance[] (applied flag, skipReason)

Needs a new OrmYamlAnnotator (similar pattern to DbtYamlAnnotator and DbtExportAnnotator) that operates at the text level after serialization to preserve YAML formatting. Should be idempotent via stripBARWISEComments().

Ties into barwise-8ud (@orm chat participant) -- when the user returns with answers, the participant could re-process and resolve the TODOs.
close_reason: Merged PR #37

- Update DraftModelParser to propagate dataType when creating object types
- Update constraint parsing to propagate isPreferred

title: Modeler-defined queries / subqueries
description:
GAP (ORM2Core.xsd: QueryType, QueryDerivationRuleType,
QueryDerivationPathType, QueryParametersType, SubqueryRef). NORMA lets a
modeler define named queries with parameters, derived over a role path,
composable via subqueries -- a derived-predicate capability beyond
individual derived fact types. barwise has no query concept.

Depends on the role-path model (sibling issue) and overlaps derivation
(5t9.2). Lower priority; may be partially out of scope -- decide during
spec. Filed so the construct is tracked rather than silently unmodeled.
acceptance_criteria: Metamodel can represent a named query (derivation path + parameters) and subquery references; importer reads QueryType/QueryDerivationRule/QueryParameters; documented scope limits.

title: Model notes / element notes (first-class annotations)
description:
GAP (ORM2Core.xsd: ModelNoteType/ModelNotesType, NoteType/NotesType,
ModelNoteReferencedByType). NORMA supports free-text notes attached to
the model or to specific elements, separate from the formal Definition.
barwise has `definition` but no general note/annotation channel, so
NORMA notes are dropped on import. Partial overlap with barwise-rqb
(user-defined custom attributes) -- reconcile during design.

Work: add an optional notes channel on the model and elements; import
ModelNote/Note; serialize. Small.
acceptance_criteria: Model and individual elements can carry free-text notes distinct from formal definitions; importer reads ModelNote/Note; round-trips through .orm.yaml.
notes: Implemented (PR pending): a free-text note channel distinct from formal definition -- OrmModel.note (model-level), ObjectType.note, FactType.note, each with config field + getter/setter, serializer round-trip (emitted when non-empty), and JSON Schema property. Additive, no orm_version bump (1.1 open). Deferred: NORMA ModelNote/Note import/export (NORMA's ModelNote + ModelNoteReferencedBy linking and the note/definition distinction need XSD verification); role-level notes (value-type/object/fact notes cover the common case). Overlap with barwise-rqb (custom attributes) noted -- notes are a single free-text channel, custom attributes would be a keyed map; complementary.

title: Role-path / join-rule model (substrate for join constraints, derivation, queries)
description:
GAP (architectural; ORM2Core.xsd: LeadRolePathType, RoleSubPathType,
JoinRuleType, ObjectUnifierType, PathedRoleType, RootObjectTypeType).
ORM 2 has a general ROLE-PATH model: a path starts at a root object type
and traverses roles across fact types, joining on shared object types,
with object unifiers merging path nodes. This substrate underlies three
things barwise lacks: join-path set-comparison/ring constraints (5t9.6),
derivation rule paths (5t9.2), and modeler-defined queries (sibling
issue). The flat role-id sequences barwise uses today cannot express it.

Work (spec-first; larger design): design the role-path representation and
its serialization, then layer the dependents on it. 5t9.6 depends on this.
acceptance_criteria: A role-path representation (lead role path from a root object type, sub-paths, joins on shared object types, object unifiers) usable by set-comparison constraints, derivation rules, and queries; importer reads LeadRolePath/JoinRule/ObjectUnifier.
notes: PR (revised representation) implemented per the canonical-research finding: JoinOperand = RolePath + projection (node indices); join_subset/equality/exclusion compare projected tuple SETS (Halpin's role-sequence-projected-from-a-join-path), not single endpoints. Updated model/serde/schema (join_operand_def)/structural validation (projection range, arity + column-type agreement)/tuple-set population evaluation/verbalization/diff. Single-column is projection length 1; PR-1's endpoint behavior is the [root,endpoint] special case. NORMA substrate (purpose tags, RolePathOwner, calculated projections) intentionally not copied.

title: Multi-role (role-sequence) frequency constraints
description:
GAP (minor). FrequencyConstraint binds a single role
(Constraint.ts:227 `readonly roleId: string`). ORM 2 frequency
constraints can range over a role SEQUENCE (a combination of roles), e.g.
"each (Room, TimeSlot) combination is booked 0..1 times". Multi-role
NORMA frequency constraints can't be represented and are dropped/narrowed.

Work: allow FrequencyConstraint to take a role-id sequence (keep single
-role as the common case); update import, verbalization, validation.
acceptance_criteria: FrequencyConstraint can span a role sequence, not just a single role; importer maps NORMA multi-role FrequencyConstraint; verbalization handles it.
close_reason: Implemented: FrequencyConstraint.roleId -> roleIds (role sequence); back-compat serialization (role/roles); per-tuple validation; multi-role verbalization; diff; NORMA multi-role round-trip included. Tier 2 complete.

title: Set-comparison and ring constraints over join paths
description:
GAP (advanced). barwise's set-comparison constraints reference flat role
-id sequences (SubsetConstraint.subsetRoles/supersetRoles,
EqualityConstraint, ExclusionConstraint). There is no join-path concept
(rg join/role-path/rolepath in core/src/model finds nothing relevant).

ORM 2 allows these constraints to range over JOIN PATHS -- role sequences
that traverse several fact types joined on shared object types (e.g. a
subset constraint between a path A-plays-B-in-FT1 and B-relates-C-in-FT2).
NORMA persists these as multi-fact RoleSequences with join semantics.
Without a join-path model, such constraints can't round-trip and complex
NORMA models lose them.

Work: design a join-path representation (ordered hops over fact types,
joined on object types) usable by subset/equality/exclusion/ring; parse
NORMA's joined RoleSequences. Larger design effort -- spec first.
acceptance_criteria: Subset/equality/exclusion (and ring) constraints can reference role sequences defined over a join path spanning multiple fact types; importer reads NORMA join-path role sequences; documented limitations for v1.
notes: XSD: set-comparison constraints use ConstraintRoleSequenceWithJoinType over the role-path model (LeadRolePath/JoinRule/ObjectUnifier). This is an instance of the general role-path substrate filed as .13; .6 depends on .13.

title: Default values on value types and roles
description:
GAP (minor). No default-value concept (rg default in Role.ts/ObjectType.ts
finds nothing). NORMA/ORM 2 allow a default value on a value type or a
role. Useful for relational mapping (SQL DEFAULT) and lost on import.

Work: optional `defaultValue?: string` on ObjectType (value types) and/or
Role; import from NORMA; thread into DDL rendering.
acceptance_criteria: Value types/roles can carry a default value; importer reads NORMA default values; relational mapping can emit DEFAULT where applicable.
notes: Implemented (PR pending): ObjectType.defaultValue (value-type default) + config + getter; serializer round-trip (default_value, emitted when present); JSON Schema property; and the DDL payoff -- Column.defaultValue, threaded by the relational mapper from the value type onto its column, rendered as SQL DEFAULT (numeric/boolean bare, else quoted with '' escaping). No orm_version bump (1.1 open). Deferred: NORMA import/export of DefaultValue -- NORMA's exact DefaultValue representation (attribute vs element, InvariantDefaultValue) needs XSD verification, like value-comparison's operator enum; folded into the NORMA-mapping follow-on. Role-level defaults also deferred (value-type default is the common/DDL-relevant case).

title: Independent (reference-independent) object types
description:
GAP. ObjectType has no `independent` flag (rg independent in
ObjectType.ts finds only an unrelated data-type comment). In ORM 2 an
INDEPENDENT object type (drawn with an open dot) may have instances that
participate in no fact other than its own reference -- i.e. it is not
existentially dependent on playing any asserted role. This matters for
reference schemes and for correct mandatory-role inference.

Work: add optional `independent?: boolean` (default false) to ObjectType,
import NORMA's IsIndependent attribute, and surface it in
verbalization/diagram. Low effort, improves fidelity.
acceptance_criteria: ObjectType can be marked independent; importer reads NORMA's independent flag; verbalization/diagram note it; default false.
notes: Implemented (PR pending): ObjectType.independent (default false) + config; serializer round-trip (emitted only when true); JSON Schema property; completeness-warning exemption (independent types are not flagged 'isolated'); and the full NORMA round-trip -- parser reads IsIndependent, mapper maps it, writer+serializer emit IsIndependent (RT-A test). No orm_version bump (1.1 already open). Remaining: diagram open-dot marker for independent types is a @barwise/diagram follow-on (verbalization has no object-type-property slot today; the ADR 'verbalizable' gate is satisfied conceptually).

Interface surface (if built):

- VS Code: export commands
acceptance_criteria: Status confirmed (present/absent). If absent and we decide to build: R2RML output validates against a reference tool (Ontop or Morph-KGC); CLI/MCP/VS Code all expose it
