title: Add sample population to an example .orm.yaml file
description:
The ORM metamodel and serialization already support sample populations (instances populating fact types, validated against constraints in real time). Per discussion: this is one of the most stakeholder-friendly features. Combined with verbalization, populations are the killer demo — 'here's the rule in English, here's data that satisfies it, here's data that violates it.'

But no example .orm.yaml currently demonstrates populations. New users and reviewers don't see the feature exists.

Scope: pick an existing example (or add a new small one), add a sample population that exercises multiple constraint types — mandatory, uniqueness, value constraint, maybe a ring constraint. Make sure validation catches deliberate violations and lets correct rows pass.

Interface surface:
- Examples/docs only — no code interface change
- But: VS Code population validation UI, CLI 'barwise validate', and MCP 'validate_model' should all light up usefully when run on the example
acceptance_criteria: Example .orm.yaml includes a populated section; running validation surfaces both passes and intentional failures; example is referenced from docs/README

title: LLM-assisted derivation rule elicitation (English to formal rule)
description:
ORM2 derivation rules define a fact type whose population is computed from other fact types (e.g., 'Person is a Senior Citizen if Person has Age > 65'). FORML (ORM's textual rule language) is limited and academically oriented.

Two complementary pieces:
1. A formal rule language as a target: likely Datalog-style over the metamodel, leveraging the symbolic query API (barwise-6i6). 'A derivation rule is a named query.'
2. LLM-assisted elicitation: user describes a rule in English; LLM translates to the formal language; user reviews and saves. Mirrors how verbalization works in the other direction.

Key principle: LLM is a translator, NOT a reasoner. The rule itself is formal and executable; the LLM just helps author it.

Interface surface:
- @barwise/core: rule language parser + evaluator (built on query API)
- CLI: 'barwise rule add' / 'barwise rule list' / 'barwise rule eval'
- MCP: 'propose_rule' tool (LLM-assisted authoring) + 'evaluate_rule' tool (execution)
- VS Code: chat agent supports 'add a derivation rule that...' workflow; rules editor in webview

Depends on barwise-6i6 (symbolic query API).
acceptance_criteria: Formal rule language documented; round-trip English -> rule -> verbalization works; evaluator produces correct derivations on test models; all three interfaces support the workflow

title: Strengthen @barwise chat to delegate deterministic questions to symbolic query API
description:
The @barwise VS Code chat agent currently answers questions about ORM models primarily via LLM prompting. For deterministic questions (what fact types involve X, what constraints reference role Y, etc.) this is wasteful and untrustworthy. The agent should delegate these to the symbolic model query API (barwise-6i6) before falling back to LLM reasoning.

Two distinct concerns to separate:
- Symbolic Q&A over the model — deterministic, no LLM needed (use query API)
- Inferred entailments, natural-language synthesis, ambiguity resolution — LLM appropriate

Pattern: the chat agent receives a question, attempts to classify it as symbolic vs reasoning, calls the appropriate path. LLM still does English-to-query translation and result narration.

Interface surface:
- VS Code: @barwise chat participant — primary consumer
- MCP: the same delegation logic should be exposed so external agents (not just VS Code) get the same benefit
- CLI: less direct, but 'barwise query' subcommand gives the same surface for scripting

Depends on barwise-6i6 (symbolic model query API).
acceptance_criteria: At least 5 representative deterministic questions answered without LLM call; chat agent transcripts show clear delegation; same behavior available via MCP

title: Reference ontology import connectors (schema.org, FIBO, QUDT)
description:
Add import connectors for well-known reference ontologies so modelers can pull in standard vocabularies rather than re-defining concepts. This addresses the 'no IRI / no linked-data' gap in ORM2 by anchoring object types and value types to external definitions.

Likely an epic with one connector per vocabulary:
- schema.org (general purpose, very broad)
- FIBO (financial industry business ontology)
- QUDT (units / quantities / dimensions)
- Possibly others (DCAT for data catalogs, FOAF, etc.)

Plugs into the FormatDescriptor registry.

Interface surface:
- CLI: 'barwise import --format=schema-org --filter=<...>' or 'barwise reference add <iri>'
- MCP: import_model tool, or a new reference_lookup tool
- VS Code: a 'browse reference ontologies' UI or command palette entry

Open design questions:
- Do we materialize imports into the .orm.yaml, or maintain references to external IRIs?
- How does this interact with our (currently absent) IRI support on object types?
acceptance_criteria: At least schema.org import works end-to-end; modeler can anchor an ORM object type to a referenced concept; CLI/MCP/VS Code all support the workflow

title: Property Graph Schema (PG-Schema) export connector
description:
Add a format connector for PG-Schema (LDBC, 2023) and/or Neo4j Cypher schema. Property graphs are the increasingly common 'I want a graph but OWL is too much' middle ground, and ORM2's attribute-free nature is a near-perfect source for property graphs.

Plugs into the FormatDescriptor registry in @barwise/core.

Interface surface:
- CLI: 'barwise export --format=pg-schema' (and possibly --format=cypher)
- MCP: existing export_model tool picks it up via the registry
- VS Code: register in export commands

Mapping notes:
- ORM object types -> node labels
- ORM binary fact types -> relationship types (or properties on nodes for single-valued)
- ORM objectified facts -> intermediate nodes
- ORM uniqueness / mandatory -> PG-Schema key / cardinality constraints
acceptance_criteria: PG-Schema output parses with a reference validator; CLI/MCP/VS Code can all invoke export

title: Write 'Splitting a model into an OrmProject' guide
description: Write a user-facing guide under docs/ that walks through converting a monolithic .orm.yaml into a multi-file OrmProject. The conceptual treatment in ARCHITECTURE.md §3.1.4 covers the metamodel but not the workflow. The guide should use auction.orm.yaml as the worked example, splitting it into catalog/auctions/payments/parties domains with context mappings between them. Cover: when to split (signals you've outgrown a single file), how to identify bounded contexts, the .orm-project.yaml manifest, .map.yaml for cross-context entity mappings, the 'context:ObjectType' qualified-ref syntax, and how to load/validate/diagram the result. Cross-link from docs/CLI.md, docs/MCP.md, and examples/README.md.
acceptance_criteria: docs/ORM_PROJECT_GUIDE.md (or similar) exists with a worked auction.orm.yaml split example; CLI.md/MCP.md/examples/README.md link to it; a new reader can follow the steps and end up with a working multi-domain project.

title: CLI tooling to scaffold an OrmProject from a monolithic model
description: Add CLI commands to make project scaffolding ergonomic. Today converting auction.orm.yaml into a multi-domain project means hand-editing YAML and cutting entities across files — error-prone and tedious. Proposed commands: (a) 'barwise project init <name>' creates a new empty .orm-project.yaml with the standard directory layout, (b) 'barwise project split <source.orm.yaml> --domains catalog,auctions,payments' interactively or by config assigns entities/facts to domains and writes the resulting files plus suggested context mappings for any entity referenced across seams. The split command should produce a valid project on output (round-trip loads cleanly) and flag entities that need manual resolution (multiple seams, ambiguous ownership).
design: Start with 'barwise project init' (trivial). For 'split', start with a config-driven mode: user supplies a YAML file mapping entity IDs to domain names, tool does the cut. Interactive/LLM-assisted modes can follow later.
acceptance_criteria: 'barwise project init' produces a valid empty project; 'barwise project split' with a config file cuts auction.orm.yaml into the 4 subdomains from the discussion and the output loads, validates, and diagrams cleanly.

title: Subdomain categorization for entities and fact types
description:
## Problem

An ORM model has a single top-level 'domain_context' string, but individual
entity types and fact types have no way to be tagged by subdomain. In a
realistic model, many entities naturally belong to sub-domains like
'transaction', 'catalog', 'customer', 'fulfillment', etc. Users need a way
to understand which parts of a model belong to which subdomain, filter
by subdomain, and answer questions like "show me everything in the
transaction domain."

## Current state

- 'OrmModel.domainContext' is a single string on the whole model
- 'ObjectType' and 'FactType' have no domain/category/tag fields
- 'OrmProject' supports multi-file models where each file has its own
  'domain_context' — that covers cross-file separation but not within-file
  subdomains
- 'DiagramLayout' can define named visual views that subset the model,
  but those are visual groupings, not semantic domain tags
- 'describeDomain' tool operates on the whole model; it does not filter
  by subdomain

## Design space to explore

1. **Explicit domain tags** — add an optional 'domain' (or 'domains: string[]')
   field to ObjectType and FactType. Simple, explicit, but puts the burden
   on the modeler to tag things consistently.
2. **Named subdomains as first-class elements** — a top-level 'subdomains'
   list, each naming a set of entity/fact type IDs. Benefits: supports
   overlapping subdomains, displayable in the sidebar, can be used for
   filtering and scoped exports.
3. **Derived clustering** — infer subdomains automatically from graph
   structure (connected components, louvain community detection, shared
   fact types). No modeler burden, but fuzzy and may not match intent.
4. **Namespace-prefixed names** — e.g. 'Transaction.Payment' → derived
   from naming convention. Discourages good naming practice.
5. **Hybrid** — first-class subdomains (option 2) with LLM-assisted
   suggestion of subdomain membership from the model structure + names.

## Downstream uses

- Sidebar: group entities/facts by subdomain
- Diagram: filter to a single subdomain (similar to named views)
- describeDomain: filter by subdomain
- Export (DDL, OpenAPI): scoped export of a subdomain
- Impact analysis: "changes to X affect which subdomains"
- LLM context: when merging incoming changes, group related elements
  by subdomain for clearer diffs and verbalizations

## Not yet decided

- Should subdomains be hierarchical (Transaction > Payment > Refund)?
- Can an entity belong to multiple subdomains? (Probably yes.)
- How do subdomains interact with the existing 'OrmProject' multi-file
  domain_context — are they nested, parallel, or the same thing?
- Is this a core metamodel change (breaks serialization) or an
  annotation layer?

## Acceptance criteria

This issue is scoping/design only. A follow-up implementation issue
will be filed once the design direction is chosen. Success here means:

- [ ] ADR-style document capturing the chosen design (under docs/)
- [ ] Serialization impact assessed (round-trip, migration path)
- [ ] Impact on downstream consumers documented (sidebar, diagram,
      describeDomain, exports)

title: Expose sidebar/diagram interactions as LLM-accessible tools
description: The sidebar and diagram now support click-to-highlight, orientation toggle, position save, and model browsing. These same capabilities should be available to LLMs via the MCP server and Copilot chat tools so that an AI assistant can navigate, highlight, and manipulate diagrams programmatically. Examples: 'highlight Customer and its connections', 'save the current diagram layout', 'list all fact types for Order'. Bridges the gap between UI-driven and conversational workflows.

title: dbt source/staging YAML generation from diagram view
description: Generate dbt source and staging model YAML from a view's entities. Column names, data types, and descriptions come from the ORM model's entity types, fact types, and definitions. Bridges conceptual modeling directly into the analytics engineering workflow.

title: Data dictionary export from diagram view
description: Generate a human-readable data dictionary document from a view's elements. Lists entities with definitions, fact type readings, constraints, and data types. Output as Markdown or HTML. Useful for stakeholder review and documentation deliverables.

title: Scoped OpenAPI export from diagram view
description: Export OpenAPI schema for only the entities in the active view. Uses the existing OpenAPI format support scoped to the view's element subset.

title: Scoped DDL export from diagram view
description: Export DDL (CREATE TABLE statements) for only the entities in the active view, not the full model. Uses the existing relational mapping engine scoped to the view's element subset.

title: View switching in sidebar
description: Extend the sidebar model browser to show named views. Click a view to open it in the diagram panel. Active view highlighted. Add/remove elements from views via right-click context menu.

title: Named diagram views with element subsets
description: Support multiple named views per model, each containing a subset of elements. Create views from: subtype tree of an entity, N-degree neighborhood, or manual selection. Auto-include fact types that connect included entities so views are structurally complete. Store view definitions in the diagrams section of .orm.yaml.
close_reason: Implemented named diagram views with element subsets. Save View button in hop bar captures current filtered elements. Views stored in .orm.yaml with element names. Clicking a saved view in the sidebar loads it. Auto-includes connecting fact types and subtype facts.

title: Multi-view diagrams with submodel export
description: Support multiple named diagram views per .orm.yaml model, each containing a subset of elements with persisted positions. Views serve as scoped lenses (e.g., a report's data requirements, a type hierarchy). Key capabilities: (1) diagrams section in .orm.yaml schema, (2) sidebar tree view for navigation, (3) add/remove elements from views, (4) auto-include connecting fact types, (5) export a view's submodel as DDL, OpenAPI, or data dictionary. Inspired by NORMA's multi-diagram support (ormDiagram:ORMDiagram sections with per-shape AbsoluteBounds). Analytic use case: model known data domains, create views scoped to reports/deliverables, identify structural gaps, export scoped artifacts.

title: LLM transcript import: emit candidate constraints with TODO annotations
description: The LLM transcript extraction currently produces fact types without constraints, even when the transcript contains clear signals like 'exactly one', 'at most one', 'identified by', etc. The LLM should infer likely uniqueness and mandatory constraints from these phrases and emit them with TODO/NOTE annotations (similar to how it already annotates ambiguous identifiers) so the modeler can confirm, adjust, or remove them. This reduces the manual work needed after import while keeping the human in the loop.

title: Verbalization on entity selection
description: When an entity type is clicked in the diagram webview, display verbalizations of its fact types in a tooltip or side panel. Reuses click handler infrastructure from the focus subset feature. Requires integration with the verbalization engine from @barwise/core.

title: Add import dbt CLI subcommand
description: Core has a fully implemented importDbtProject() pipeline for reverse-engineering ORM models from dbt projects but no CLI subcommand exposes it. Add 'BARWISE import dbt <dir>' with directory scanning and schema.yml parsing. Estimated ~100 lines.

title: Add export avro CLI subcommand
description: Core has a fully implemented renderAvro() function for generating Apache Avro schema definitions (.avsc JSON) but no CLI subcommand exposes it. Add 'BARWISE export avro' following the same pattern as export dbt and export openapi. Estimated ~50 lines.

title: Add import norma CLI subcommand
description: Core has a fully implemented importNormaXml() pipeline (parse, map, validate) for importing NORMA .orm XML files but no CLI subcommand exposes it. Add 'BARWISE import norma <file>' following the same pattern as import transcript. Estimated ~70 lines.

title: Extract objectified fact types from transcripts
description: The metamodel supports objectified fact types (a fact type that is also treated as an entity type, e.g. 'Enrollment' objectifying 'Student enrolls in CourseOffering'). The extraction prompt and DraftModelParser do not handle objectification. The LLM could be instructed to identify when a relationship is itself referenced as an entity in other fact types.
close_reason: PR #63 merged. Objectification extraction added to LLM pipeline.

title: Extract frequency constraints from transcripts
description: The metamodel supports frequency constraints ('each X plays this role at least N / at most M times', e.g. 'each Employee works on at least 1 and at most 3 Projects'). Not handled in extraction.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #57

title: Extract ring constraints from transcripts
description: The metamodel supports ring constraints (irreflexive, asymmetric, intransitive, acyclic, etc. on self-referencing fact types, e.g. 'a Person cannot manage themselves' or 'the reports-to hierarchy must be acyclic'). Not handled in extraction.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #57

title: Extract equality constraints from transcripts
description: The metamodel supports equality constraints (two role populations are equal, e.g. 'a Person teaches a Course if and only if that Person is qualified for that Course'). Not handled in extraction.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #57

title: Extract subset constraints from transcripts
description: The metamodel supports subset constraints (one role population is a subset of another, e.g. 'every Person who manages a Department must also work in that Department'). Not handled in extraction.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #57

title: Extract exclusive-or constraints from transcripts
description: The metamodel supports exclusive_or (exactly one of several roles must be played -- combines disjunctive mandatory + exclusion, e.g. 'every Payment is exactly one of CashPayment, CardPayment, or CheckPayment'). Not handled in extraction.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #57

title: Extract exclusion constraints from transcripts
description: The metamodel supports exclusion constraints (two role combinations cannot both occur, e.g. 'a Person cannot be both a Student and an Instructor in the same Department'). Not handled in extraction prompt, ExtractionTypes, or DraftModelParser.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #57

title: Extract disjunctive mandatory constraints from transcripts
description: The metamodel supports disjunctive_mandatory (at least one of several roles must be played, e.g. 'every Person must have either a Phone or an Email'). Not handled in extraction prompt, ExtractionTypes, or DraftModelParser.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #57

title: Extract external uniqueness constraints from transcripts
description: The metamodel supports external_uniqueness (uniqueness spanning roles across multiple fact types, e.g. composite identification of CourseOffering by Course+Semester). The extraction prompt and DraftModelParser do not handle this constraint type. ExtractionTypes.ts InferredConstraintType only allows internal_uniqueness | mandatory | value_constraint.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #57

title: Stale TODO for OrderStatus value constraint that was actually applied
description: Line 4 of order-management.orm.yaml has a TODO saying 'Role-level value constraints from transcripts are not yet supported' for OrderStatus, but the value constraint was successfully applied on the OrderStatus value type (lines 68-73). The TODO is stale/incorrect -- the annotator should not emit it when the constraint was handled at the value-type level.
close_reason: Already resolved -- annotator no longer emits stale TODO for value constraints applied at value-type level. Historical transcript files retain the old output as versioned snapshots.

title: Excessive positional-fallback NOTE annotations
description: The OrmYamlAnnotator emits 17 NOTE annotations saying 'could not precisely match role ... Using positional fallback' for constraints that were all correctly applied. The constraint matcher should recognize common patterns (e.g., role names matching object type names) to reduce noise. The positional fallback itself works fine but the volume of notes drowns out important annotations.
close_reason: Resolved by v0.2.2 prompt improvements. LLM now produces constraint role references that the annotator matches without positional fallback.

title: Add OpenAPI export format
description: Add an OpenAPI (Swagger) export format that generates an OpenAPI 3.x spec from an ORM model. Entity types become schemas, fact types inform endpoints and relationships. Would extend the existing export command alongside dbt, ddl, and avro formats.
close_reason: PR #64 merged. OpenAPI 3.0 export format added to core renderer and CLI.

title: MCP docs: add GitHub Copilot setup instructions
close_reason: PR #62 merged -- GitHub Copilot setup instructions added to docs/MCP.md

title: Review internal naming for semantic precision
description:
Audit internal type names, field names, and variable names across all packages for semantic precision. A project that models business domains using ubiquitous language should hold itself to the same standard internally.

Known examples to review:
- MergeValidationResult.errors -- field name is accurate (contains only errors) but the comment and usage patterns could be clearer
- Survey all exported interfaces and types for naming that could mislead consumers

Scope: @BARWISE/core public API surface first, then internal naming, then downstream packages.
close_reason: PR #60 merged -- all 5 renames landed across 23 files in 4 packages

title: Annotate dbt export output with TODO/NOTE comments
description: When rendering dbt exports (schema.yml, model SQL) from an ORM model via renderDbt(), inject TODO/NOTE comments for gaps in the model that affect the export quality. Examples: missing column descriptions, ambiguous constraint mappings, value types without data_type (defaulting to TEXT), fact types without readings. Follows the same pattern as DbtYamlAnnotator used for dbt imports.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #33

title: Import dbt project and infer ORM facts from project structure
description: Reverse-engineer an ORM 2 model from an existing dbt project. Infer entity types from model names, fact types from relationships (refs), value types from column definitions, and constraints from tests (unique, not_null, accepted_values). Identify gaps where dbt structure is ambiguous -- e.g. missing reference modes, unclear cardinality, denormalized columns that could represent multiple facts. Output should be a draft OrmModel that a modeler can refine, with completeness warnings highlighting what could not be inferred.
close_reason: Implemented dbt project importer with parser, mapper, gap report, and 39 tests

title: Add validation warnings for dataType and isPreferred consistency
description:
Add two completeness/consistency validation rules:

1. Warn when a value type has no dataType set. Value types should ideally declare their conceptual data type so the relational mapper can generate accurate column types instead of defaulting to TEXT.

2. Warn when an entity type has zero isPreferred uniqueness constraints (no preferred identifier declared) or more than one isPreferred constraint (conflicting preferred identifiers). This helps catch modeling issues early.

These are non-blocking warnings (not errors) in the validation engine, similar to existing completeness warnings in src/validation/rules/completenessWarnings.ts.

Files to modify:
- src/validation/rules/completenessWarnings.ts (or new file)
- tests/validation/completenessWarnings.test.ts
close_reason: Implemented two completeness warning rules for dataType and isPreferred consistency

title: Extend population validation to Phase 2 constraints (subset, ring, exclusion, equality)
close_reason: Implemented population validation for all 5 Phase 2 constraint types with 26 new tests

title: Integration exports (dbt, CI/CD validator, MCP server)
description: Build downstream integration points: dbt model export, CI/CD validation CLI, and MCP server for external tool access. These are separate projects that consume @BARWISE/core.
close_reason: Implemented dbt and Avro renderers alongside existing DDL renderer

title: Decide VS Code Validate command vs LSP project diagnostics
description: Once the LSP handles .orm-project.yaml, the Validate Model command is largely redundant; decide deprecation. Deferred from barwise-r4f; see docs/specs/orm-project-surface-wiring.spec.md.

title: Dynamic (state-transition) rules
description:
GAP / SCOPE QUESTION (ORM2Core.xsd: DynamicRuleType, DynamicRuleNodeState,
GeneralRuleType). NORMA supports dynamic rules that constrain state
transitions (before/after model states), as opposed to the static
constraints barwise models. This is exotic and arguably outside barwise's
conceptual-modeling scope.

Filed at low priority as a decision: either model it or record it as an
explicit non-goal so the omission is intentional, not accidental.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded on whether barwise models dynamic rules; if yes, metamodel + importer support DynamicRuleType; if no, documented as an explicit non-goal.

title: test issue (will delete)
description: test write
close_reason: Closed

title: NORMA XML diagram export (round-trip)
description: Export barwise models back to NORMA .orm XML format, including diagram positions. Combined with existing NORMA import, this enables round-trip interop with the NORMA toolset.

title: Visual lineage: entity usage across views
description: Show where an entity appears across all named views. Provides cross-view lineage for understanding how entities connect across different business contexts. Supports impact analysis -- if an entity changes, which views are affected.

title: Research OMG Ontology and EKG standards for barwise integration
description: Investigate connections between ORM 2 fact-based modeling and OMG working groups: (1) OMG Ontology group (https://www.omg.org/ontology/) and (2) Enterprise Knowledge Graphs (https://www.omg.org/ekg/, https://ekgf.org/). ORM's situation semantics foundation may align well with ontological and knowledge graph representations. Explore whether barwise could serve as a bridge between business-readable ORM models and these standard formats. This is exploratory research, not implementation.

title: Add standalone merge command to CLI
description: Core has mergeModels() and mergeAndValidate() for three-way model merging, but it is only used implicitly during 'import transcript' when an existing file is present. Add a standalone 'BARWISE merge <base> <ours> <theirs>' command for collaborative modeling workflows. Estimated ~70 lines.

title: Extract aliases/synonyms from transcripts
description: The metamodel supports aliases on ObjectType (synonym tracking). The extraction prompt does not instruct the LLM to identify when stakeholders use different names for the same concept and record them as aliases (vs. flagging as ambiguity). Example: 'Client' and 'Customer' used interchangeably could be captured as aliases.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #65 — aliases extracted from LLM transcripts and passed through to OrmModel

title: Add @orm Copilot Chat participant for guided domain elicitation
description:
Implement a VS Code Chat Participant (@orm) that guides users through structured ORM domain elicitation directly in the Copilot Chat panel.

The participant would:
- Walk users through a structured interview (identify entities, reference modes, relationships, cardinality, constraints, value restrictions)
- Build the ORM model incrementally with feedback at each step
- Use the existing LLM extraction pipeline from @BARWISE/llm
- Generate the .orm.yaml file when the conversation is complete
- Solve both the 'what questions to ask' and 'import the result' problems in a single interaction

This is more impactful than a static conversation guide because it lives where engineers already work (Copilot Chat panel), provides real-time guidance, and doesn't require a separate transcript import step.

Requires the VS Code Chat Participant API (vscode.chat.createChatParticipant).
